Title: Ecology-guided Bacillus SynCom from a rice–duckweed core reveals division of labor for concurrent growth promotion and sheath blight suppression

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use as a microbial functional marker to select or engineer Bacillus inoculants with auxin-producing capacity for root growth promotion. Best deployed in multi-strain consortia rather than alone, because the paper shows division of labor and resilience in SynComs. Couple with strains carrying antimicrobial and siderophore pathways. Caution: genetic necessity was not validated by knockout/complementation in this study; field robustness remains untested.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Association with increased plant-growth-promoting metabolite output, especially auxin-related activity, contributing to rice root growth promotion in the SynCom context.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation; qRT-PCR in SynCom and single-strain dropout communities; untargeted LC-MS metabolite profiling correlated with IAA-related metabolites.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse culture; rice-duckweed co-cultivation as ecological source system; LB co-culture of Bacillus SynCom; pathogen challenge with Rhizoctonia solani in downstream assays.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not applicable in rice; gene assayed in Bacillus cultures within SynCom/dropout conditions.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In the SynCom context, auxin-biosynthesis-linked expression was maintained in full SynCom and sharply decreased in the -D16 dropout, indicating dominant expression contribution from strain D16.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA/BioProject: PRJNA1296676); untargeted LC-MS metabolomics (no accession reported); qRT-PCR (no accession reported)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper interprets ipdC as part of tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis potential in Bacillus MAGs and SynCom strains; functional causality remains correlative.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No phytotoxicity detected at community level; single-gene tradeoffs not assessed.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Indole-3-pyruvate decarboxylase family; thiamine pyrophosphate-dependent decarboxylase
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Bacillus SynCom member(s), primarily strain D16
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ipdC was identified in Bacillus MAGs and SynCom genomes as a plant-beneficial auxin-biosynthesis marker. 2. qRT-PCR showed strong loss of ipdC expression specifically in the -D16 community, indicating D16 is the principal auxin-specialist strain. 3. This auxin-linked module contributed to IAA-associated metabolite accumulation and rice growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes root elongation and plant growth

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a screening marker for Bacillus strains with tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis potential in bioinoculant development. Best stacked with biocontrol-metabolite producers in a consortium. Caution: no direct gene knockout or biochemical complementation was performed.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to auxin-linked plant growth promotion in the SynCom, associated with higher IAA-related metabolite abundance and improved rice root development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation; qRT-PCR in SynCom vs dropout communities; LC-MS metabolite association.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-derived ecological selection; bacterial co-culture in LB; downstream rice growth assays and pathogen challenge system.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not applicable in rice; expression examined in Bacillus SynCom cultures.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression dropped sharply in -D16, indicating dominant source from strain D16 in the consortium.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (PRJNA1296676); untargeted LC-MS metabolomics; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper groups ipdC/iaaM together as auxin biosynthesis markers; exact pathway architecture in each Bacillus strain was not fully resolved.
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Bacillus SynCom member(s), primarily strain D16
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. iaaM was identified in core Bacillus genomes linked to auxin biosynthesis. 2. Its transcript level collapsed in the -D16 community, indicating D16 as the irreplaceable auxin-expression source. 3. This gene module aligned with IAA-associated metabolite accumulation and plant growth promotion by the full SynCom.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulates root growth and plant vigor

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as a microbial marker for selecting strains with nitrogen-fixation support traits in rice biofertilizer consortia. Should be paired with auxin, siderophore, and antifungal metabolite modules because nitrogen-related benefit alone does not explain full SynCom performance. Caution: actual N-fixation contribution in planta was not directly quantified.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with nitrogen-fixation capacity within the SynCom, contributing to plant-growth-promoting functionality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trait assay for nitrogen fixation; qRT-PCR of nif genes across dropout communities; genome/MAG functional annotation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial culture under assay conditions; greenhouse rice assay context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not applicable in rice; assayed in Bacillus SynCom cultures.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: nifH expression collapsed mainly in the -D11 community, indicating D11 as exclusive nif-operon contributor.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents nif genes as strain-specific, with D11 being the key source; in planta nitrogen transfer was not measured.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nifH marked the nitrogen-fixation module detected in Bacillus functional screening. 2. qRT-PCR showed near-complete loss of nifH expression in the -D11 community. 3. D11 therefore provides a nonredundant nitrogen-fixation-related function within the SynCom.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial intracellular/extracellular nitrogen-fixation interface in rhizosphere context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nitrogen nutrition and growth

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for selecting nonredundant nitrogen-fixing Bacillus members in multifunctional SynComs. Use with growth-promoting and biocontrol strains for trait stacking.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports the nitrogen-fixation transcriptional module of the SynCom.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR across dropout communities; functional trait screening; metagenomic annotation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture and greenhouse validation context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression strongly dependent on presence of D11.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Correlative evidence only; no isotope tracing of nitrogen transfer.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nifD belongs to the D11-dependent nitrogen fixation module. 2. Transcription collapsed in -D11. 3. Indicates exclusive contribution of D11 to this PGP function.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth by improving nitrogen availability

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful molecular marker for Bacillus nitrogen-fixation module selection in inoculant design.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the D11-specific nitrogen-fixation expression program linked to PGP activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; nitrogen-fixation phenotypic assay; metagenomic annotation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial culture conditions in SynCom/dropout system.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct in planta flux validation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nifK was part of the D11-dependent nif module. 2. Expression was near abolished when D11 was omitted. 3. Supports nonredundant nitrogen-fixation function in the consortium.

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good screening marker for siderophore-producing Bacillus strains in iron-mobilizing bioinoculants. Likely most valuable when combined with auxin and antimicrobial modules. Caution: pathway redundancy suggests single-gene targeting may not abolish function at community level.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with siderophore biosynthesis and iron mobilization contributing to plant growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation; qRT-PCR of biosynthetic genes; LC-MS metabolite profiling including bacillibactin; siderophore plate assays.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial culture in SynCom/dropout conditions; greenhouse rice assay context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: dhbA expression remained largely stable across most dropouts, indicating distributed redundancy among multiple strains.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to dhbACEBF module in MAGs and SynCom genomes; bacillibactin levels were only modestly reduced across most dropouts.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: D09/D10/D11/D12/D14/D16/D17 at least one or more strains; exact distribution not fully resolved
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dhbA is part of the bacillibactin siderophore module enriched in the duckweed-derived Bacillus core. 2. Expression remained broadly stable across most dropout treatments, supporting functional redundancy. 3. This redundancy likely underpins resilient iron-mobilization capacity of the SynCom.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mobilizes iron, supporting microbial fitness and plant nutrient acquisition

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for phosphate-solubilizing bacteria in SynCom design. Since expression remained stable across dropouts, community-level redundancy should be exploited rather than relying on a single strain.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with phosphate turnover/solubilization-related plant growth-promoting function.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trait screening for phosphate solubilization; qRT-PCR; genome annotation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial culture and greenhouse rice assay context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Largely stable across single-strain deletions, suggesting redundancy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene assignment to phosphate turnover is based on authors’ qRT-PCR panel and trait context; exact enzymatic role per strain not dissected.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm / membrane-associated depending on enzyme form
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gcd was included among PGP genes linked to phosphate-related functions. 2. Its expression showed broad stability across most dropouts. 3. This supports partial redundancy for phosphate-solubilization capacity in the SynCom.

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for stress resilience of Bacillus consortia; suitable for selecting robust inoculants for fluctuating environments. Best used with direct biocontrol pathways.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports oxidative and nitrosative stress buffering of the community, indirectly contributing to resilience and sustained biocontrol/PGP outputs.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR heatmap across SynCom and dropouts.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture context; environmental robustness interpretation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Generally stable in SynCom; reduced especially in -D10 and -D11.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Stress-response genes are not direct nutrient-metabolite determinants but support community robustness.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. katA contributes to oxidative stress tolerance in the SynCom. 2. Expression declined mainly in -D10 and -D11, indicating disproportionate contribution of those strains. 3. Stress buffering likely supports stable multifunctionality under community conditions.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances microbial survival and community resilience

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value marker/module for engineering Bacillus biocontrol inoculants against sheath blight. Best stacked with fengycin, bacillomycin, and difficidin pathways to broaden antimicrobial spectrum and resilience. Because D14/D17 were major contributors, prioritize strains carrying strong srf clusters. Caution: direct knockout validation is proposed but not yet performed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Nonribosomal assembly of the cyclic lipopeptide surfactin from amino acid residues and a fatty acid starter via NRPS modules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes antimicrobial metabolite accumulation and contributes to suppression of Rhizoctonia solani.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: antiSMASH genome/MAG identification; qRT-PCR of srfAA and related genes; untargeted LC-MS annotation of surfactin-like features; strain dropout phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; NRPS assembly-line enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms an antimicrobial end product contributing to disease suppression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture in LB; greenhouse rice pathogen challenge downstream.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Surfactin nonribosomal peptide synthetase subunit A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in full SynCom; strongly reduced when D14 or D17 was removed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors discuss srfAA-AD cluster; exact subunit-by-subunit strain distribution not fully tabulated in the main text.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential metabolic burden not assessed; no phytotoxicity observed at community level.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Amino acid residues; fatty acyl starter; ATP-dependent activated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key antimicrobial biosynthetic module; bottleneck not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacillus SynCom strains, principally D14 and D17 contributors
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. srfAA was detected in Bacillus core genomes and expressed in the SynCom. 2. Its expression and surfactin-like metabolite abundance dropped substantially when D14 or D17 was omitted. 3. This identifies D14/D17 as principal lipopeptide contributors to disease suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant / plant-associated extracellular milieu
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses pathogens and may aid colonization or induced resistance

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate marker for selecting sheath-blight suppressive Bacillus strains. Should be pyramided with surfactin, fengycin, and difficidin pathways for multi-mode antifungal action.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Nonribosomal lipopeptide assembly producing bacillomycin-class antifungal molecules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to antifungal chemistry and R. solani suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: antiSMASH BGC prediction; qRT-PCR of bamA-E; LC-MS annotation of bacillomycin-like features; dropout comparison.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; NRPS assembly-line enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture and pathogen challenge context.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in SynCom; reduced 80-90% when D14 or D17 was removed according to discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main text also references bacillomycin D-like signals declining in -D14.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Amino acid residues and fatty acyl starter units
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control module for bacillomycin production in major producer strains
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacillus SynCom strains, especially D14 and D17
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bamA-E clusters were among core Bacillus antimicrobial pathways. 2. Their transcripts fell strongly upon loss of D14 or D17. 3. Bacillomycin production is therefore a principal, strain-specialized antifungal module in the SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular culture medium / rhizosphere or phyllosphere-associated milieu
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal suppression of sheath blight pathogen

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful antifungal pathway module for SynCom construction against filamentous pathogens. Best combined with surfactin and bacillomycin for broader biocontrol efficacy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Nonribosomal lipopeptide assembly leading to fengycin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports disease-suppressive metabolite pool and antifungal activity against R. solani.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: antiSMASH identification; qRT-PCR of fenA-E; LC-MS annotation of fengycin-like signals; dropout metabolomics.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; NRPS assembly-line enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture and pathogen challenge conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with D14/D17-centered lipopeptide module in network analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes fengycin-like features among major antimicrobial chemistries recovered uniquely or strongly in complete SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Amino acids; fatty acyl starter units
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway module, not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacillus SynCom strains linked mainly to D14 and D17
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fenA-E were present in SynCom-associated Bacillus genomes. 2. Fengycin metabolites formed part of the complete SynCom antimicrobial portfolio. 3. The pathway belongs to a strain-partitioned disease-suppression module centered on lipopeptide-producing members.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important module for broad-spectrum disease suppression; prioritize in Bacillus strains intended for pathogen control. Can be stacked with lipopeptide producers for complementary chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polyketide synthase-mediated assembly of the macrocyclic polyene antibiotic difficidin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to antimicrobial metabolite production and pathogen suppression in SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: antiSMASH PKS cluster identification; qRT-PCR of dfn genes/pksX; LC-MS annotation of difficidin; strain dropout analysis.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; modular PKS system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial co-culture in vitro and greenhouse pathogen challenge context.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Difficidin pathway relied predominantly on D16; dfn/pksX transcripts sharply lost in -D16.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion explicitly states difficidin relied predominantly on D16.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Malonyl-CoA-derived extender units and starter acyl units
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway module in D16-centered antimicrobial branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dfnA-E/pksX-type PKS genes were detected in core Bacillus genomes. 2. Their expression and associated antimicrobial output depended strongly on D16. 3. This establishes a D16-specific polyketide arm complementing lipopeptide-mediated biocontrol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity for disease suppression

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polyketide/secondary metabolite biosynthesis linked to difficidin-type module
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for selecting PKS-positive Bacillus biocontrol strains. Combine with NRPS pathways for robust antifungal consortia.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Modular polyketide extension and assembly generating antibiotic polyketide scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports disease-suppressive chemistry in the SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; modular PKS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms/helps form antimicrobial end product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bacterial culture and pathogen challenge context.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly associated with D16 and sharply reduced in -D16.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper lists pksX among hallmark Bacillus functional markers.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key biosynthetic module; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pksX belongs to the D16-centered antimicrobial pathway set. 2. Its transcript abundance dropped sharply in the -D16 community. 3. This module complements lipopeptide pathways to generate broad-spectrum biocontrol output.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Select for bacillibactin-positive strains to enhance iron mobilization and competitiveness in rhizosphere inoculants. Since function is partly redundant, use multispecies/multistrain designs to ensure robustness.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembly of the catecholate siderophore bacillibactin from 2,3-dihydroxybenzoate and glycine/threonine-derived units.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports iron mobilization and plant-growth-promoting metabolite profile.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: antiSMASH/KEGG pathway annotation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS detection of bacillibactin; siderophore assays.
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not a single EC number; siderophore NRPS-related assembly system
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms an iron-chelating metabolite that supports nutrient acquisition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: bacillibactin output was only modestly reduced in most dropouts, suggesting distributed production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper lists both dhb and bacABCDE modules in Bacillus genomes/MAGs.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; community function appears redundant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bacA-associated siderophore biosynthesis was enriched in the Bacillus core and present in SynCom genomes. 2. Bacillibactin was detected among representative PGP metabolites. 3. Production was distributed across multiple strains, supporting robust iron-mobilization function.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful module for engineering growth-promoting Bacillus inoculants, especially for early root stimulation. Combine with auxin pathway and siderophore traits for stronger PGP. Caution: D10 contributes to volatile biosynthesis but is not the main auxin hub.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate-derived intermediates to acetolactate in the first committed step toward acetoin and 2,3-butanediol volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports growth-promoting volatile production within the SynCom metabolome.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Network inference from metabolomics and qRT-PCR; dropout-associated metabolite reductions.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for growth-promoting volatile end products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Connected mainly to D10 in the integrated network.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream in acetoin/2,3-butanediol branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted LC-MS; qRT-PCR; integrated network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed in network section rather than full gene-by-gene results table.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step-like role for volatile branch, not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. alsS was linked to D10-specific volatile biosynthesis in the integrated network. 2. This module contributed to acetoin/2,3-butanediol-related growth-promotion chemistry. 3. It complements, rather than replaces, the D16-centered auxin module.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture supernatant and headspace-associated milieu
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and root development

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for selecting Bacillus strains producing growth-promoting volatiles. Pair with auxin producers and antifungal strains in multifunctional SynComs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces acetoin to 2,3-butanediol in the terminal step of volatile alcohol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to SynCom-derived growth-promoting volatile output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated network inference from LC-MS and qRT-PCR/dropout patterns.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a target growth-promoting volatile product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated mainly with D10 in the integrated network.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted LC-MS; qRT-PCR; integrated network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 2,3-BDO was among key PGP metabolites recovered by full SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bdhA was connected to D10 in the integrated network. 2. It underlies 2,3-butanediol production contributing to PGP chemistry. 3. This volatile-producing branch complements D16-centered auxin production in the SynCom.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances plant growth and root development
